source: ESPN FIFA World Cup + per-player news feeds

items.headline: Every team's championship, group odds for the 2026 World Cup
items.description: Odds for every team to advance from its group through the knockout stage and to win the 2026 World Cup.

items.headline: England's Reece James misses training ahead of Panama World Cup clash
items.description: Reece James missed training ahead of England's final group game against Panama.

items.headline: Sources: Norway's Erling Haaland to miss France World Cup clash
items.description: Erling Haaland will start Norway's World Cup Group I clash against Kylian Mbappe's France on the substitutes' bench with coach Stale Solbakken planning to rest ten players, sources have told ESPN.

items.headline: World Cup Daily LIVE: Yamal gets WWE belt, France vs. Norway in Group I
items.description: We continue with the final days of the 2026 FIFA World Cup group stage as we figure out who will qualify for the knockout stage.

items.headline: Ghana's players are fully on board with 'father' Carlos Queiroz and his defensive style
items.description: Ghana are one of only four teams -- along with Mexico, Argentina and Spain -- not to concede in their two opening World Cup games, despite facing 30 shots across their matches and using two goalkeepers.

items.headline: Ronwen Williams pays tribute to past Bafana greats after South Korea win
items.description: Ronwen Williams said that although he was the first captain of a Bafana team which made the World Cup knockout rounds, past icons paved the way.

items.headline: Aubrey Modiba: Canada must beware of Bafana 'upset' despite 'home' advantage
items.description: South Africa's Aubrey Modiba is quietly confident that Bafana Bafana can add to the World Cup's growing list of surprise results when they face Canada in their Last 32 match in Inglewood on Sunday.

items.headline: South Africa vs. Canada - Kick-off time, team news, how to watch World Cup round of 32 clash
items.description: Bafana Bafana will face Canada in their first ever FIFA World Cup knockout game on Sunday in Los Angeles, and Themba Zwane remains suspended.

items.headline: The Dream Factory: Inside the 'cruel' system behind Argentina's soccer empire
items.description: An investigation into Argentina's soccer system that produced the defending World Cup champions found that it is rife with exploitation.

items.headline: Black Stars wearing 'Atletico Ghana' tag as a badge of honour as World Cup knockouts beckon
items.description: Ghana's Marvin Senaya has responded to critics of the Black Stars' new-look playing style under head coach Carlos Queiroz by insisting that the players are only concerned about the team's results as they close in on a spot in the World Cup knockouts.

items.headline: Croatia vs. Ghana: Kick-off time, team news, permutations, how to watch
items.description: Croatia and Ghana will face off in a World Cup clash which could have implications for not only the Black Stars, but also England.

items.headline: Jesse Marsch, Alistair Johnston warn of South Africa threat ahead of Canada's World Cup round of 32 test
items.description: Canada coach Jesse Marsch and defender Alistair Johnston say South Africa will provide a stern test in Sunday's FIFA World Cup round of 32 match at SoFi Stadium in Inglewood, California.

items.headline: Socceroos World Cup Daily: Popovic says every Socceroo has a role
items.description: Socceroos staff will need to make changes to the starting XI thanks to injuries ahead of Thursday's crunch clash with Paraguay.

items.headline: USMNT World Cup bracket scenarios, odds to advance, predicted path to knockouts
items.description: Who will Team USA face in the World Cup knockout rounds? What's the likely path? What are the odds? What do projection models say?

items.headline: Mohamed Salah finally writes World Cup history with Egypt - can he salvage his international legacy?
items.description: Mohamed Salah has achieved more in the sport than any other Egyptian player in history, gracing the upper echelons of the game with Liverpool, winning silverware aplenty at Anfield, and establishing himself as a global superstar.

items.headline: What's it like to face a young phenom like Messi, Bellingham?
items.description: With Lionel Messi and Jude Bellingham starring at the World Cup, here are firsthand accounts from those who got an early glimpse of greatness.

items.headline: Morgan Rogers: Jude Bellingham and I could play together for England
items.description: Morgan Rogers says he and close friend Jude Bellingham could "100%" thrive alongside each other in the same England starting lineup.

items.headline: 2026 World Cup June 26 schedule: Soccer games today
items.description: What is the World Cup schedule today? Find out more about Friday's matches.

items.headline: Fans kick on after teen arrested at Socceroos live site
items.description: Police were out in force as thousands of fans flocked to Federation Square to watch Australia take on Paraguay at the World Cup.

items.headline: World Cup 2026: Are we witnessing the end of this great Senegal team?
items.description: It's not over, not by any stretch: they have the quality, the firepower to defeat Iraq by a large margin and give themselves a chance to progress.

items.headline: Éder Militão warns Brazil of threat posed by Japan
items.description: Éder Militão has warned his Brazil teammates to expect a big test against Japan in Monday's World Cup round of 32 showdown in Houston.

items.headline: World Cup 2026: Can Lionel Messi and Cristiano Ronaldo finally meet on biggest stage?
items.description: In a sixth World Cup for Lionel Messi and Cristiano Ronaldo, could the pair finally face each other in the tournament?

items.headline: World Cup: Grades for every eliminated team, as they exit the tournament
items.description: When the World Cup ends, 47 teams will have been eliminated, and we're grading every one on how their run compared with expectations.

items.headline: Why Algeria vs. Austria is 2026 World Cup's biggest grudge match
items.description: It's been 44 years in the making: Algeria have a shot at revenge on Austria for the "Disgrace of Gijón," the match that changed the World Cup forever.

items.headline: Pochettino hits back at reporters for not congratulating USA on topping group
items.description: Pochettino hits back at reporters for not congratulating USA on topping group

items.headline: Sweden's Anthony Elanga frustrated after Japan draw: 'I didn't know it was enough'
items.description: The 48-team format of the World Cup is proving to be quite the puzzle to work out the permutations of third-place qualifiers, even for some of the players.

items.headline: Pochettino puzzled by media's questions after U.S. wins group
items.description: Mauricio Pochettino was combative and seemingly frustrated with the media during his postgame news conference Thursday night, telling reporters after the United States lost to Türkiye 3-2 that the team still won the group.

items.headline: 2026 FIFA World Cup match schedule: Fixtures, results, features
items.description: Check out all the fixtures, features and results for the 2026 FIFA World Cup in the United States, Mexico and Canada.

items.headline: Pulisic looks ready to make his mark on USMNT's World Cup right when it needs him
items.description: Christian Pulisic's experience vs. Türkiye mirrored that of the U.S. team: The beginning was terrific. The ending stunk. And none of it matters nearly as much as what comes next.

items.headline: Ecuador declares public holiday to celebrate victory over Germany at World Cup
items.description: Ecuador president Daniel Noboa declared Friday a public holiday in the country following the national team's come-from-behind win against Germany at the World Cup.

items.headline: 'Best left-back in our history': Bos praised
items.description: After a standout display against Paraguay, Jordan Bos was hailed by teammates, with Miloš Degenek declaring him Australia's best left-back in history, Joey Lynch writes.

items.headline: Eberechi Eze backs 'special player' Bukayo Saka to unlock Panama defence
items.description: Eberechi Eze says Bukayo Saka is a "special player" as his England and Arsenal team-mate hopes to make his first start at the World Cup against Panama on Saturday.

items.headline: 'Bless him!' - Potter on Elanga's confusion around Sweden progressing to knockouts

items.headline: Are the Netherlands ready for Morocco in the knockouts?
items.description: Lizzy Becherano reports on the Netherlands defeating Tunisia, setting up a matchup with Morocco in the round of 32.

items.headline: Behind the banner: How a World Cup-themed USA tifo was born
items.description: Soccer tifos have been a staple at games since the 1960s. Here's how USA supporters created the storied fan art for a 2025 game as a World Cup sendoff.

items.headline: Christian Pulisic subs on as U.S. loses to Türkiye on last kick
items.description: After starting the World Cup with two performances full of incredible verve and vibrancy, the United States finished its group stage with a showing that was erratic and inconsistent in a match that had little meaning.

items.headline: Lewis Ferguson: Scotland 'let ourselves down' in World Cup campaign
items.description: Lewis Ferguson has said he was left with a feeling that Scotland "let ourselves down a bit" after their World Cup Group C campaign.

items.headline: Lineker, Shearer, Owen, Rooney, Kane - is England's near half-century of world-class strikers about to end?
items.description: With an elite striker having worn the Three Lions on their chest for 42 years, England have taken the position for granted - but is Harry Kane the last?

items.headline: Meet the Bindons: The first mother-son duo to appear in a World Cup
items.description: Tyler Bindon followed in his mother Jenny's footsteps by appearing for New Zealand in this year's World Cup. ESPN talks to Jenny to understand their journey in the sport.

items.headline: Panama scouting report: What can England expect from the World Cup minnows?
items.description: ESPN talks to Panamian journalist Luis Arauz to get the lowdown on England's final group stage opponents.

items.headline: Job done! Socceroos ease into 2026 FIFA World Cup knockouts
items.description: It might not have been one for the purists, but the Socceroos' draw with Paraguay is a significant result that should not be underrated.
